strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Kansas (KU) is a public R1 flagship research university located in Lawrence, Kansas, and a member of the prestigious Association of American Universities (AAU). It offers over 345 degree programs across 14 schools and is highly recognized for its robust programs in special education, nursing, business, and engineering [cite: 1]. Offering an accessible admissions pathway alongside a vibrant Big 12 sports culture, KU balances expansive research opportunities with a quintessential college town experience.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Numbers-focused Assured Admission with Holistic Review for competitive programs

analyst_takeaways.insight: KU offers 'assured admission' based purely on GPA and standardized test thresholds, making general admission highly predictable and accessible (93%+ acceptance rate) [cite: 2, 3].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students meeting the minimum metrics are guaranteed a spot in the College of Liberal Arts & Sciences, though specialized schools (like Nursing and Engineering) require secondary, more competitive reviews.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university provides generous, automatic merit scholarships based primarily on a student's unweighted or weighted GPA (up to $64,000 over four years for out-of-state students) [cite: 4].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants with strong academic records can easily calculate their expected merit aid, making KU a highly transparent and predictable financial safety for out-of-state applicants.

analyst_takeaways.insight: KU maintains a 69% 6-year graduation rate, which is slightly below average for flagship universities [cite: 5, 6].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students must be proactive in seeking academic advising and support, as navigating the large university environment and internal transfer requirements requires self-advocacy.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Campus life is heavily influenced by a strong Big 12 sports culture, particularly basketball at Allen Fieldhouse, and an active Greek life with over 500 student organizations [cite: 7, 8, 9].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students seeking a spirited, traditional American college town experience will find Lawrence an ideal fit.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and traditional Big 12 college experience in the highly-rated college town of Lawrence. Campus features over 500 student organizations, a prominent Greek system (15% men, 25% women participate), and a unifying, legendary basketball culture [cite: 7, 8, 10].
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: The expansive campus spans North, Central, and West areas. The city/campus bus system provides free, comprehensive public transit connecting major buildings, residential halls, and downtown Lawrence [cite: 8, 11].
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As an AAU member with over $466 million in annual research expenditures, undergraduates have ample opportunities to engage in high-level research across disciplines, particularly in biosciences, engineering, and paleontology [cite: 1, 9].

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for general admission. Students are assured admission with a 3.25+ GPA or an ACT of 21+/SAT of 1060+ alongside a 2.0+ GPA. Submitted scores are used for Math/English placement and competitive professional school decisions [cite: 2, 12].

longitudinal_trends.notes: KU does not have a waitlist policy for first-time freshmen applicants [cite: 13].
KU does not have a waitlist policy for first-time freshmen applicants [cite: 11].

gpa.notes: GPA is heavily used for assured admissions and automatic merit scholarship qualification [cite: 12].

testing.policy_note: 8.6% submitted SAT scores; 55.2% submitted ACT scores [cite: 12, 14].

colleges.name: College of Liberal Arts & Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students meeting the assured admission criteria are automatically admitted. Ideal for exploratory students and those seeking standard BA/BS tracks [cite: 2].
colleges.housing_profile: Access to general university-owned co-ed residence halls, scholarship halls, and living-learning communities [cite: 10].
colleges.student_archetype: Broad academic interests, pre-med, or exploratory.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: One of the most popular majors on campus, resulting in over 350 graduates annually [cite: 15, 16].

majors.name: Biology
majors.notes: Standard track for pre-med students. Introductory biology and chemistry courses are noted by students as difficult 'weed-out' classes [cite: 17].

colleges.name: School of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly competitive. Applicants must meet strict math readiness standards (ACT Math 28+) and class rank thresholds for direct admission [cite: 18].
colleges.housing_profile: General housing with engineering-specific living-learning communities available.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Direct admission requires graduating in the top half of high school class and a mathematics score of 28+ on the ACT [cite: 18].
majors.notes: Transfer students from other institutions or KU units generally need a 2.5+ GPA to be considered for admission [cite: 18].

colleges.name: School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students should use the Business Index Pathway (ACT/10 + GPA = 5.55+) or achieve a 3.4+ HS GPA for direct admission [cite: 19].
colleges.student_archetype: Future corporate leaders, aspiring accountants and finance professionals.

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.special_requirements: Admission is conditional upon receiving final official transcripts and/or official standardized test scores [cite: 19].
majors.notes: A highly popular school at KU, known for its strong experiential learning and accounting/finance outcomes [cite: 16, 20].

colleges.name: School of Nursing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students must complete 60 prerequisite hours with a high GPA before applying for the clinical BSN portion at the KC or Salina campuses [cite: 21].
colleges.student_archetype: Dedicated healthcare aspirants willing to maintain strict GPA thresholds.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Requires 60 credit hours of specific prerequisites. Admission is highly competitive and holistic, reviewing health-related volunteer experience, leadership, and personal characteristics [cite: 21].
majors.notes: Program cohorts are strictly capped at 180 students in Kansas City and 30 in Salina [cite: 21].

application_deadlines.notes: KU operates on rolling admissions but has a priority date of August 1 and a final closing date of August 11 for Fall [cite: 22].
Priority deadline to apply to the University Honors Program and to maximize consideration for freshman scholarships [cite: 4, 23, 24].

supplemental_requirements.details: The university does not require supplemental essays for general admission, making it accessible for quick applications [cite: 22, 25, 26].
Portfolio and essay are submitted online directly to the Department of Design [cite: 19].

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: General admission is purely formulaic (GPA/Test score thresholds) via Assured Admission. Holistic review is only triggered if a student falls below assured metrics or applies to competitive schools like Design, Nursing, or University Honors [cite: 12, 27].

major_selection_tactics: Meet direct admit thresholds for specialized schools (Business, Engineering) to avoid internal transfer hurdles later.
If you fall short of Business or Engineering requirements, apply to the College of Liberal Arts & Sciences to establish a KU GPA before attempting an internal transfer.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: KU allows students to select an alternate major. Because the College of Liberal Arts & Sciences is highly accessible, students rejected from competitive schools (like Engineering) are frequently offered admission to their alternate choice or as an undeclared student [cite: 28].

financials.tuition_model: Per credit hour with flat standard rates and differential tuition by program/school.

scholarships.name: KU Freshman Scholarships
scholarships.benefits: Renewable four-year scholarship up to $67k for out-of-state students.

scholarships.name: Jerry Bailey Educational Opportunity Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: One-time award for low-income and first-generation KU students.

scholarships.name: Transfer Impact Award
scholarships.benefits: Renewable six-semester scholarship for students transferring with a minimum 3.5 cumulative GPA.

ap_policy.general_rule: KU awards course credit for qualifying AP scores, not a grade. Students may still take the exam even if they didn't take the specific AP course.
exceptions: Credit earned for both AP English Literature and AP English Language exams is considered repeat credit [cite: 29].
ap_policy.usage: Satisfies specific KU Core 34 requirements or allows placement into higher-level coursework [cite: 29, 30, 31].

ib_policy.general_rule: Awards credit based on the specific subject and whether the exam was Higher Level (HL) or Standard Level (SL) [cite: 29].

transfer_articulation.restrictions: KU does not award transfer credit based on ACE or Dantes recommendations. Maximum 75 credits transferrable from a two-year institution [cite: 22, 29].

what_it_takes: Meeting the clear-cut GPA and ACT/SAT thresholds for Assured Admission guarantees entry to the main college [cite: 2].
For competitive programs like BSN Nursing, maintaining a very high college GPA in 60 credits of rigorous science prerequisites is vital [cite: 21].
Applying by the December 1 priority deadline to lock in automatic merit scholarships [cite: 23].
common_activities: Attending Kansas Jayhawks basketball games at Allen Fieldhouse.
Participating in Greek Life (15% men, 25% women) [cite: 10].
Relaxing or studying by Potter Lake [cite: 8].
Shopping and socializing in Downtown Lawrence [cite: 9].
Engaging with the 500+ registered student organizations via Rock Chalk Central [cite: 32].
essay_tips: Because KU does not require a general supplemental essay, any optional essays (or essays for specific programs like Design or Honors) should be highly specific to your background and how it prepares you for that specific cohort [cite: 19, 24].
Do not rely on the essay to bypass the strict GPA/Test requirements for Assured Admission.
red_flags: Falling below the 2.5 college GPA threshold when attempting to transfer internally into Engineering or Nursing.
Lacking 'Kansas ties' while holding borderline stats when applying to the highly selective KU School of Medicine [cite: 33].
insights: Lawrence is universally praised as an elite 'college town' with a strong symbiotic relationship between the municipality and the university [cite: 9, 34].
The administration is highly focused on scalable advising and proactive interventions to boost graduation rates and address 'weed-out' culture [cite: 35].
